     6	
     7	    # @param user [User]
     8	    # @param article_params [Hash]
     9	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :title
    10	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :body_markdown
    11	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :main_image
    12	    # @option article_params [Boolean] :published
    13	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :description
    14	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :video_thumbnail_url
    15	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :canonical_url

    17	    # @option article_params [Integer, NilClass] :collection_id
    18	    # @option article_params [Boolean] :archived
    19	    # @option article_params [String<Array>] :tags
    20	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone] :published_at
    21	    def initialize(user, article_params)
    22	      @user = user
    23	      @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    def call
    27	      rate_limit!
    28	
    29	      create_article.tap do
    30	        subscribe_author if article.persisted?
    31	        refresh_auto_audience_segments if article.published?
    32	        complete_onboarding_first_post if article.published?
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    private
    37	
    38	    attr_reader :article, :user, :article_params
    39	
    40	    def normalize_params(original_params)
    41	      original_params.except(:tags).tap do |params|
    42	        # convert tags from array to a string
    43	        if (tags = original_params[:tags]).present?
    44	          params[:tag_list] = tags.join(", ")
    45	        end
    46	      end
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    def rate_limit!
    50	      rate_limit_to_use = if user.decorate.considered_new?
    51	                            :published_article_antispam_creation
    52	                          else
    53	                            :published_article_creation
    54	                          end
    55	
    56	      user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(rate_limit_to_use)
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    def refresh_auto_audience_segments
    60	      user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    def complete_onboarding_first_post
    64	      return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
    65	
    66	      user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    def create_article
    70	      begin
    71	        @article = Article.new(article_params) do |article|
    72	          article.user_id = user.id
    73	          article.show_comments = true
    74	        end
    75	      rescue ArgumentError => e
    76	        if e.message.include?("is not a valid type_of")
    77	          @article = Article.new(article_params.except(:type_of)) do |article|
    78	            article.user_id = user.id
    79	          end
    80	          @article.errors.add(:type_of, :invalid)
    81	          return @article
    82	        else
    83	          raise e
    84	        end
    85	      end
    86	
    87	      # Set collection after creation to avoid it being cleared by evaluate_front_matter
    88	      # which clears collection_id when title is present in frontmatter
    89	      if @article.save
    90	        found_series = series
    91	        if found_series.present?
    92	          @article.update_column(:collection_id, found_series.id)
    93	          @article.association(:collection).reset
    94	        end
    95	      end
    96	      @article
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def series
   100	      @series ||= if article_params[:series].blank?
   101	                    nil
   102	                  else
   103	                    organization = article_params[:organization_id].present? ? Organization.find_by(id: article_params[:organization_id]) : nil
   104	                    Collection.find_series(article_params[:series], user, organization: organization)
   105	                  end
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    # Subscribe author to notifications for all comments on their article.
   109	    def subscribe_author
   110	      NotificationSubscription.create(user: user,
   111	                                      notifiable: article,
   112	                                      config: "all_comments")
   113	    end
   114	  end
   115	end
===== services/articles/updater.rb =====
     1	module Articles
     2	  class Updater
     3	    Result = Struct.new(:success, :article, keyword_init: true)
     4	
     5	    def self.call(...)
     6	      new(...).call
     7	    end
     8	
     9	    def initialize(user, article, article_params)
    10	      @user = user
    11	      @article = article
    12	      @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def call
    16	      user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(:article_update)
    17	      success = article.update(article_params)
    18	
    19	      if success
    20	        user.rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:article_update)
    21	
    22	        if became_unpublished?
    23	          remove_all_notifications
    24	          bust_author_profile_cache
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers if remains_published?
    28	        refresh_auto_audience_segments if became_published?
    29	        complete_onboarding_first_post if became_published?
    30	      end
    31	
    32	      Result.new(success: success, article: article.decorate)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    private
    36	
    37	    attr_reader :user, :article, :article_params
    38	
    39	    def normalize_params(original_params)
    40	      article_params = original_params.dup
    41	
    42	      # updated edited time only if already published and not edited by an admin
    43	      update_edited_at = article.user == user && article.published
    44	
    45	      # remove published_at values received from a user if an articles was published before (has past published_at)
    46	      # published_at will remain as it was in this case
    47	      article_params.delete :published_at if article.published_at && !article.scheduled?
    48	
    49	      # NOTE: It's surprising that this is article.user and not @user
    50	      Articles::Attributes.new(article_params, article.user, organization: article.organization)
    51	        .for_update(update_edited_at: update_edited_at)
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    def refresh_auto_audience_segments
    55	      user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    def complete_onboarding_first_post
    59	      return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
    60	
    61	      user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def became_published?
    65	      article.published? && !article.published_previously_was
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    def remains_published?
    69	      article.published && article.saved_change_to_published.blank?
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    def became_unpublished?
    73	      article.saved_changes["published"] == [true, false]
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    # If the article has already been published and is only being updated, then we need to create
    77	    # mentions and send notifications to mentioned users inline via the Mentions::CreateAll service.
    78	    def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers
    79	      Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    # Remove any associated notifications if Article is unpublished
    83	    def remove_all_notifications
    84	      Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article",
    85	                                                      action: "Published")
    86	      ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article",
    87	                                    action: "Published")
    88	
    89	      if article.comments.exists?
    90	        Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids,
    91	                                notifiable_type: "Comment")
    92	      end
    93	      return unless article.mentions.exists?
    94	
    95	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.mentions.ids,
    96	                              notifiable_type: "Mention")
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def bust_author_profile_cache
   100	      EdgeCache::BustUser.call(article.user)
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	end

     3	    # The default number of days old that an article can be for us
     4	    # to consider it in the relevance feed.

    34	    # @param user [User]

file.content:
module Mentions
  # This class creates mentions + associated notifications for Articles and Comments.
  # This class will check to see if there are any @-mentions in the post, and will
  # create the associated mentions inline if necessary.
  class CreateAll
    def self.call(...)
      new(...).call
    end

    def initialize(notifiable)
      @notifiable = notifiable
    end

    def call
      mentioned_users = users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author

      delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(mentioned_users)
      create_mentions_for(mentioned_users)
    end

    private

    def users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
      mentioned_usernames = extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text

      collect_existing_users(mentioned_usernames)
        .then do |existing_mentioned_users|
          reject_notifiable_author(existing_mentioned_users)
        end
    end

    def collect_existing_users(usernames)
      User.registered.where(username: usernames)
    end

    def create_mentions_for(users)
      users.each { |user| create_mention_for(user) }
    end

    def extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
      # The "mentioned-user" css is added by Html::Parser#user_link_if_exists
      doc = Nokogiri::HTML(notifiable.processed_html)

      # Remove any mentions that are embedded within any liquid tags
      non_liquid_tag_mentions = doc.css(".mentioned-user").reject do |tag|
        tag.ancestors(".liquid-comment").any? ||
          tag.ancestors("[class^=ltag]").any? ||
          tag.ancestors("[class*=' ltag']").any?
      end

      non_liquid_tag_mentions.map { |link| link.text.delete("@").downcase }
    end

    def reject_notifiable_author(users)
      users.reject { |user| authored_by?(user, notifiable) }
    end

    def authored_by?(user, notifiable)
      user.id == notifiable.user_id
    end

    def delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(users)
      mentions = notifiable.mentions.where.not(user_id: users).destroy_all
      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: mentions.map(&:id), notifiable_type: "Mention") if mentions.present?
    end

    def user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
      user.notifications.exists?(notifiable_id: notifiable.id, notifiable_type: "Comment")
    end

    def create_mention_for(user)
      # Do not create additional notifications for being mentioned in a comment.
      if notifiable.is_a?(Comment) && user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
        return
      end

      # The mentionable_type is the model that created the mention, the user is the user to be mentioned.
      mention = Mention.create(user_id: user.id, mentionable_id: notifiable.id,
                               mentionable_type: notifiable.class.name)

      # If notifiable is an Article, we need to create the notification for the mention immediately so
      # that the notification exists in the database before we attempt to create other Article-related notifications.
      # However, if notifiable is a Comment, we can create the notification for the mention in the background.
      case notifiable
      when Article
        Notification.send_mention_notification_without_delay(mention)
      when Comment
        Notification.send_mention_notification(mention)
      end

      mention
    end

    attr_reader :notifiable
  end
end

=== find_featured_story published ===
      #       the origin of this method's inner logic never included a
      #       consideration for `featured == true`.  Perhaps that
      #       should change?  This has been reported in
      #       https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/15613 and impacts
      #       Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery
      def self.call(stories, must_have_main_image: true)
        featured_story =
          if must_have_main_image
            if stories.is_a?(ActiveRecord::Relation)
              stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
            else
              stories.detect { |story| story.main_image.present? }
            end
          else
            stories.first
          end

        featured_story || Article.new
      end
    end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full audit.

---

# Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Forem is the open-source Rails (Rails 8-prep) community platform that powers dev.to. It's a large monolith with a Preact-first frontend. Standard Rails layout under `app/` with heavy use of service objects, Sidekiq workers, and Pundit policies.

**Code organization (`app/`):**
- `models/` — ActiveRecord models; cross-cutting behavior in `models/concerns/`; Article-specific helpers in `models/articles/` (e.g. `cached_entity.rb`, `feeds/`).
- `services/` — business logic (e.g. `services/articles/` lifecycle: `creator`, `updater`, `destroyer`, `unpublish`; `services/edge_cache/` Fastly busting; `services/feeds/`, `services/notifications/`, `services/mentions/`).
- `workers/` — Sidekiq jobs (`workers/articles/`, `workers/notifications/`, `workers/reactions/`, etc.).
- `queries/`, `decorators/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`, `controllers/` — request + read paths.
- `lib/data_update_scripts/` — one-off historical data migrations.

**The central content model** is `Article` at `app/models/article.rb:1` (1,858 lines) — what readers publish; comments, reactions, tags, mentions, notifications, page views and feeds all attach to it.

**Shared pieces it is built on (concerns), all under `app/models/concerns/`:**
- `Reactable` (`reactable.rb:1`) — `has_many :reactions`
- `Taggable` (`taggable.rb:1`) — cached-tag scopes + `tags_array` sync
- `UserSubscriptionSourceable` (`user_subscription_sourceable.rb:1`) — `has_many :user_subscriptions`
- `WebpageTrackable` (`webpage_trackable.rb:1`) — `has_many :webpage_references` + destroy hooks
- `LiquidEmbeddable` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:1`) — `has_many :liquid_embed_references`
- `AlgoliaSearchable` (`algolia_searchable.rb:1` → `algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb:1`) — search indexing
- `Purgeable` (`purgeable.rb:1`) — Fastly `purge`, pulled in app-wide via `ApplicationRecord`
- Plus `PgSearch::Model`, `CloudinaryHelper`, `ActionView::Helpers`, and `Articles::CachedEntity` (`app/models/articles/cached_entity.rb:1`).

Note: `Trackable` (`concerns/trackable.rb`) exists but **Article does NOT include it** — only `WebpageTrackable` (`article.rb:10`).

# Step 2 — The Article contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/article.rb:1`.

**Concerns included (`article.rb:2-10`):** `LiquidEmbeddable`, `CloudinaryHelper`, `ActionView::Helpers`, `Reactable`, `Taggable`, `UserSubscriptionSourceable`, `PgSearch::Model`, `AlgoliaSearchable`, `WebpageTrackable`. Plus `acts_as_taggable_on :tags` (`:12`) and `resourcify` (`:13`).

**`belongs_to`:** `collection` optional (`:44`, note: `touch:true` deliberately removed to avoid deadlock), `organization` optional (`:46`), `user` (`:47`), `subforem` optional (`:48`). `counter_culture :user` and `:organization` (`:50-51`).

**`has_many` / `has_one` — the teardown contract (this is the heart of the ticket):**
- `discussion_lock` — `dependent: :delete` (`:168`)
- `mentions` (polymorphic) — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:170`)
- **`comments` (polymorphic) — `dependent: :nullify`** (`:171`) — comments survive, lose their article link
- `context_notifications` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:172`); plus `context_notifications_published` scoped assoc (`:174`)
- `ai_audits` — `dependent: :nullify` (`:173`)
- `feed_events` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:176`)
- `notification_subscriptions` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:177`)
- `notifications` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:178`)
- `page_views` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:179`)
- `article_activity` — `dependent: :delete` (`:180`)
- `polls` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:183`, cascades poll votes/options/skips)
- `profile_pins` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:184`)
- `rating_votes` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:187`, relies on counter_culture)
- `tag_adjustments` — **NO `dependent:` option** (`:188`) — orphan risk on teardown
- `context_notes` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:189`), `accepts_nested_attributes_for` (`:190`)
- `trend_memberships`/`trends` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:192-193`)
- `concept_memberships`/`concepts` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:195-196`)
- Five comment-quality scoped assocs: `top_comments`, `more_inclusive_top_comments`, `recent_good_comments`, etc. (`:198-241`)
- **From concerns:** `reactions` `dependent: :destroy` (`reactable.rb:5`); `user_subscriptions` `dependent: :nullify` (`user_subscription_sourceable.rb:7`); `webpage_references` `dependent: :delete_all` (`webpage_trackable.rb:5`); `liquid_embed_references` `dependent: :destroy` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:5`).

**Enums:** `type_of` (full_post/status/fullscreen_embed) (`:126`); `automod_label` 15-value moderation enum (`:132`) with `AUTOMOD_SCORE_ADJUSTMENTS` (`:150`).

**Serialized/derived state:** `serialize :cached_user`/`:cached_organization` as YAML (`:386,:390`), set by `set_cached_entities` → `Articles::CachedEntity`; `ATTRIBUTES_CACHED_FOR_RELATED_ENTITY` (`:30`); a PG trigger maintains `reading_list_document` tsvector (`:359`); `pg_search_scope :search_articles` (`:396`); `has_neighbors :semantic_embedding` pgvector (`:329`).

**Key published-facing scopes a dependent relies on:** `published` (`published: true AND published_at <= now`) (`:411`), `unpublished` (`:415`), `featured` (`:553`), `from_subforem` (`:428`), `feed` (`:555`), `limited_column_select`/`minimal_feed_column_select`/`limited_columns_internal_select` (`:488,:499,:510`), `cached_tagged_with` (`taggable.rb:6`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** (1) the per-association `dependent:` strategy is the teardown contract — `:nullify` on comments and `:nullify` on user_subscriptions/ai_audits intentionally leave rows behind; `tag_adjustments` has *no* strategy. (2) Destroy and most writes fan out async work via callbacks (Step 3). (3) `cached_user`/`cached_organization` are denormalized snapshots feeding search + feeds. (4) The `published` scope, not just a boolean, defines visibility everywhere.

# Step 3 — How an article + attached data are handled on change

**Trigger entry points:** `Articles::Creator` (`app/services/articles/creator.rb:26`), `Articles::Updater` (`updater.rb:15`), `Articles::Unpublish` → Updater (`unpublish.rb:14`), `Articles::Destroyer` (`destroyer.rb:5`). Controllers call these (`articles_controller.rb`, API controllers).

**Inline (synchronous) on save — callbacks in `article.rb`:**
- `before_save`: `set_cached_entities` (`:305`), `set_all_dates` (`:306`), `calculate_base_scores` (`:308`), `set_caches` (path/cached_user) (`:310`), `detect_language` (`:311`); `Taggable#sync_tags_array` (`taggable.rb:57`).
- `before_destroy :before_destroy_actions, prepend` (`:313` → `:1408`): inline `bust_cache(destroying:true)` then enqueues `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker` for sibling articles. `WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains` also prepended (`webpage_trackable.rb:8`).
- `after_save`: `bust_cache` (`:316` → `:1663`: `purge` Fastly inline + `async_bust` + touch actor), `collection_cleanup` (`:317`), `generate_social_image` (`:318`), `generate_context_notes` (`:319`).
- `after_update_commit`: `update_notifications` (`:321` → `Notification.update_notifications`), `update_notification_subscriptions` (`:324`), `update_dependent_embeds_if_key_info_changed` (`:341`), `regenerate_summary_if_content_changed` (`:343`).
- `Updater` inline post-save: `Mentions::CreateAll.call` (`updater.rb:79`), `remove_all_notifications` + `EdgeCache::BustUser` on unpublish (`:83,:100`), `refresh_auto_audience_segments` (`:54`).
- `Destroyer` inline post-destroy: `Notification.remove_all` for the article and its (now-nullified) comments (`destroyer.rb:15-19`), `EdgeCache::BustUser` (`:21`).

**Asynchronous (Sidekiq) — fanned out from `after_commit`:**
- `async_score_calc` → `Articles::ScoreCalcWorker` (`:338` → `:1122`)
- `touch_collection`, `enrich_image_attributes` → `Articles::EnrichImageAttributesWorker` (`:1700`), `detect_code_block_languages` → `Articles::DetectCodeBlockLanguagesWorker` (`:1706`)
- `enqueue_generate_embedding` → `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker` (`:334`/`:1808`)
- `recompile_organization_pages` → `Organizations::RecompilePagesWorker` (`:345`/`:1729`)
- `bust_cache` → `async_bust` → `Articles::BustCacheWorker` (`:1692` → `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call`, `bust_cache_worker.rb:8`)
- `create_conditional_autovomits` → `Articles::HandleSpamWorker` (`:1682`)
- `generate_social_image` → `Images::SocialImageWorker` (`:1675`)
- `WebpageTrackable`: `SyncWebpageReferencesWorker` on update, `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker` on destroy (`webpage_trackable.rb:15,24`)
- `LiquidEmbeddable`: `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:28`)
- `AlgoliaSearchable`: `AlgoliaSearch::SearchIndexWorker` via `trigger_sidekiq_worker` (`searchable_article.rb:37`)

**Effect layers:** caches/Fastly (`EdgeCache::BustArticle` `services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb:9` — purges article, user, org, home/tag/profile pages); feeds (score recompute, org page recompile); notifications (`update_notifications`, `Mentions::CreateAll`); counters (`counter_culture :user/:organization`); search (Algolia + pg tsvector trigger + semantic embedding).

# Step 5 — How article identity & visibility are guarded

When background/cross-service code operates on an article, three layers of guarding appear:

**1. Existence (record still present).** Async workers fetch by id and bail if gone — the standard pattern after a destroy:
- `articles/bust_cache_worker.rb:5-6` (`find_by(id:)` + `return unless article`), and identically `score_calc_worker.rb:9`, `handle_spam_worker.rb:9`, `enrich_image_attributes_worker.rb:9`, `detect_code_block_languages_worker.rb:11`, `generate_summary_worker.rb:14`, `generate_context_note_worker.rb:9`, `generate_freeform_context_note_worker.rb:10`, `rating_votes/assign_rating_worker.rb:9`, `follows/update_points_worker.rb:9`, `notifications/milestone_worker.rb:9`, `notifications/subforem_change_notification_worker.rb:9`, `update_user_interest_embedding_worker.rb:8`.
- `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call` guards `return unless article` (`bust_article.rb:10`).
- `Images::SocialImageWorker:8` uses `find` (raises if gone) — note: less defensive than the `find_by` pattern.

**2. Current + published (visibility).** The `published` scope is the canonical visibility gate: `published: true AND published_at <= Time.current` (`article.rb:411`). Built on by every feed/query: `feeds/basic.rb:13`, `feeds/latest.rb:11`, `feeds/tag.rb:13`, `homepage/articles_query.rb:43`, `active_threads_query.rb:28`, `email_digest_article_collector.rb:63`, `sitemaps_controller.rb:26`, `metrics/record_daily_usage_worker.rb:8`. Explicit `published?` re-checks before acting:
- `generate_article_embedding_worker.rb:7` (`article.published?`), `articles/update_page_views_worker.rb:38` (`article&.published?`), `concepts/classify_record_worker.rb:14` (`!record.published?` → skip), `slack/messengers/article_published.rb:10` (`published && published_at > 10.minutes.ago`), `edge_cache/bust_article.rb:50` (`return unless article.published` before tag-page bust).

**3. Search/index eligibility.** `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle#indexable` (`searchable_article.rb:42`): `published && score.positive? && published_at < Time.current` — and `indexable_changed?` (`:46`) gates re-indexing. Semantic embedding has its own gate: `eligible_for_semantic_embedding?` (`article.rb:1026`) + the `after_commit` `published? && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present?` (`:336`).

**4. Authorization (request path).** `ArticlePolicy` — `manage?` requires `record.published? && !record.scheduled?` (`article_policy.rb:73`); `revoke_publication?` requires `@record.published?` (`:92`); `feed?`/`create?`/`update?` (`:40,:53,:66`).

**Identity nuances:** `scheduled?` (`article.rb:663`: `published_at && published_at > now`) distinguishes future-dated; `cached_user`/`cached_organization` (`set_cached_entities` `:1615`) snapshot the actor's identity into the article so feeds/search don't re-query. Destroyer relies on comment ids being captured *before* destroy because `comments` are `:nullify`'d (`destroyer.rb:11`).

---

# Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

The ticket changes "how it stores and tears down attached data" — i.e. the association set and their `dependent:` strategies. Ranked by risk:

**🔴 Highest — silent data loss or orphans on teardown:**
1. **`comments` `dependent: :nullify`** (`article.rb:171`). Destroyer *depends* on this: it caches `article.comments.ids` (`destroyer.rb:11`) before destroy specifically because comments survive, then removes their notifications (`:17-19`). Any change to `:destroy`/`:delete_all` silently breaks `Destroyer`, `lib/data_update_scripts/20200825095512_nullify_orphaned_comments_by_article.rb`, and `Notification.remove_all` for comments. Re-verify `spec/services/articles/destroyer_spec.rb`.
2. **`tag_adjustments` with no `dependent:`** (`article.rb:188`). Already orphans on destroy; if the rework adds cascade, must confirm `TagAdjustment` has no counter_culture/callbacks that need it, and check `tag_adjustment_creation_service.rb`.
3. **`reactions` `dependent: :destroy`** (`reactable.rb:5`) + `counter_culture`. Destroying reactions triggers per-reaction callbacks and counter sync; `reaction_counter_sync_worker.rb:80`, `reactions/update_relevant_scores_worker.rb`, `Reactable#sync_reactions_count` all assume the current relationship.
4. **`rating_votes`/`polls` `dependent: :destroy`** (`article.rb:183,:187`) — comments in code explicitly note they're `:destroy` *because* of counter_culture/cascade; switching strategy loses tallies.
5. **`before_destroy_actions` + `BustMultipleCachesWorker`** (`article.rb:1408`) — runs `prepend: true` and reads `user.article_ids`/`organization.article_ids`; reordering associations or destroy hooks can change what's busted.

**🟠 High — async fan-out keyed on the article after commit:**
6. `EdgeCache::BustArticle` (`bust_article.rb:9`) — purges article/user/org/home/tag/profile; depends on `tag_list`, `published`, `user`, `organization` still being readable.
7. `WebpageTrackable` destroy hooks (`webpage_trackable.rb:8,9`) — `capture_webpage_domains` (prepend) + `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker`; if `webpage_references` strategy changes, domain scores drift.
8. `LiquidEmbeddable` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:5,28`) + `update_dependent_embeds_worker.rb` — embeds referencing the article.
9. `notifications`/`notification_subscriptions`/`context_notifications` `:delete_all` (`article.rb:177,178,172`) — `Updater#remove_all_notifications` (`updater.rb:83`) and `Destroyer` (`destroyer.rb:15`) both also remove notifications manually; double-handling means a strategy change can leave dangling notification rows.
10. Search: `AlgoliaSearchable` (`searchable_article.rb`) + pg `reading_list_document` trigger (`article.rb:359`) + semantic embedding (`:329,:334`) — index entries must be removed/updated on teardown.

**🟡 Medium — read/derive paths that assume associations load:**
11. Feeds/ranking (`feeds/*`, `homepage/articles_query.rb`, `active_threads_query.rb`) — rely on `published`, `featured`, counters, `cached_tag_list`.
12. AI assessors (`services/ai/*`), badges (`services/badges/*`), digest (`email_digest_article_collector.rb`), analytics (`analytics_service.rb`, `admin/*`).
13. `concept_memberships`/`trend_memberships` `:destroy` (`article.rb:192,195`) + concept workers.

**Must re-verify after the change (regardless of area):** `Articles::Destroyer`, `Articles::Updater` (unpublish path), `EdgeCache::BustArticle`, all `dependent:`-affected child models' counters, the orphan-cleanup data scripts, Algolia + pg-trigger + embedding indexing, and `before_destroy_actions` ordering (`prepend: true` interacts with `WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains`).

result:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full audit.

---

# Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Forem is the open-source Rails (Rails 8-prep) community platform that powers dev.to. It's a large monolith with a Preact-first frontend. Standard Rails layout under `app/` with heavy use of service objects, Sidekiq workers, and Pundit policies.

**Code organization (`app/`):**
- `models/` — ActiveRecord models; cross-cutting behavior in `models/concerns/`; Article-specific helpers in `models/articles/` (e.g. `cached_entity.rb`, `feeds/`).
- `services/` — business logic (e.g. `services/articles/` lifecycle: `creator`, `updater`, `destroyer`, `unpublish`; `services/edge_cache/` Fastly busting; `services/feeds/`, `services/notifications/`, `services/mentions/`).
- `workers/` — Sidekiq jobs (`workers/articles/`, `workers/notifications/`, `workers/reactions/`, etc.).
- `queries/`, `decorators/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`, `controllers/` — request + read paths.
- `lib/data_update_scripts/` — one-off historical data migrations.

**The central content model** is `Article` at `app/models/article.rb:1` (1,858 lines) — what readers publish; comments, reactions, tags, mentions, notifications, page views and feeds all attach to it.

**Shared pieces it is built on (concerns), all under `app/models/concerns/`:**
- `Reactable` (`reactable.rb:1`) — `has_many :reactions`
- `Taggable` (`taggable.rb:1`) — cached-tag scopes + `tags_array` sync
- `UserSubscriptionSourceable` (`user_subscription_sourceable.rb:1`) — `has_many :user_subscriptions`
- `WebpageTrackable` (`webpage_trackable.rb:1`) — `has_many :webpage_references` + destroy hooks
- `LiquidEmbeddable` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:1`) — `has_many :liquid_embed_references`
- `AlgoliaSearchable` (`algolia_searchable.rb:1` → `algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb:1`) — search indexing
- `Purgeable` (`purgeable.rb:1`) — Fastly `purge`, pulled in app-wide via `ApplicationRecord`
- Plus `PgSearch::Model`, `CloudinaryHelper`, `ActionView::Helpers`, and `Articles::CachedEntity` (`app/models/articles/cached_entity.rb:1`).

Note: `Trackable` (`concerns/trackable.rb`) exists but **Article does NOT include it** — only `WebpageTrackable` (`article.rb:10`).

---

# Step 2 — The Article contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/article.rb:1`.

**Concerns included (`article.rb:2-10`):** `LiquidEmbeddable`, `CloudinaryHelper`, `ActionView::Helpers`, `Reactable`, `Taggable`, `UserSubscriptionSourceable`, `PgSearch::Model`, `AlgoliaSearchable`, `WebpageTrackable`. Plus `acts_as_taggable_on :tags` (`:12`) and `resourcify` (`:13`).

**`belongs_to`:** `collection` optional (`:44`, note: `touch:true` deliberately removed to avoid deadlock), `organization` optional (`:46`), `user` (`:47`), `subforem` optional (`:48`). `counter_culture :user` and `:organization` (`:50-51`).

**`has_many` / `has_one` — the teardown contract (this is the heart of the ticket):**
- `discussion_lock` — `dependent: :delete` (`:168`)
- `mentions` (polymorphic) — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:170`)
- **`comments` (polymorphic) — `dependent: :nullify`** (`:171`) — comments survive, lose their article link
- `context_notifications` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:172`); plus `context_notifications_published` scoped assoc (`:174`)
- `ai_audits` — `dependent: :nullify` (`:173`)
- `feed_events` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:176`)
- `notification_subscriptions` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:177`)
- `notifications` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:178`)
- `page_views` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:179`)
- `article_activity` — `dependent: :delete` (`:180`)
- `polls` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:183`, cascades poll votes/options/skips)
- `profile_pins` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:184`)
- `rating_votes` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:187`, relies on counter_culture)
- `tag_adjustments` — **NO `dependent:` option** (`:188`) — orphan risk on teardown
- `context_notes` — `dependent: :delete_all` (`:189`), `accepts_nested_attributes_for` (`:190`)
- `trend_memberships`/`trends` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:192-193`)
- `concept_memberships`/`concepts` — `dependent: :destroy` (`:195-196`)
- Five comment-quality scoped assocs: `top_comments`, `more_inclusive_top_comments`, `recent_good_comments`, etc. (`:198-241`)
- **From concerns:** `reactions` `dependent: :destroy` (`reactable.rb:5`); `user_subscriptions` `dependent: :nullify` (`user_subscription_sourceable.rb:7`); `webpage_references` `dependent: :delete_all` (`webpage_trackable.rb:5`); `liquid_embed_references` `dependent: :destroy` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:5`).

**Enums:** `type_of` (full_post/status/fullscreen_embed) (`:126`); `automod_label` 15-value moderation enum (`:132`) with `AUTOMOD_SCORE_ADJUSTMENTS` (`:150`).

**Serialized/derived state:** `serialize :cached_user`/`:cached_organization` as YAML (`:386,:390`), set by `set_cached_entities` → `Articles::CachedEntity`; `ATTRIBUTES_CACHED_FOR_RELATED_ENTITY` (`:30`); a PG trigger maintains `reading_list_document` tsvector (`:359`); `pg_search_scope :search_articles` (`:396`); `has_neighbors :semantic_embedding` pgvector (`:329`).

**Key published-facing scopes a dependent relies on:** `published` (`published: true AND published_at <= now`) (`:411`), `unpublished` (`:415`), `featured` (`:553`), `from_subforem` (`:428`), `feed` (`:555`), `limited_column_select`/`minimal_feed_column_select`/`limited_columns_internal_select` (`:488,:499,:510`), `cached_tagged_with` (`taggable.rb:6`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** (1) the per-association `dependent:` strategy is the teardown contract — `:nullify` on comments and `:nullify` on user_subscriptions/ai_audits intentionally leave rows behind; `tag_adjustments` has *no* strategy. (2) Destroy and most writes fan out async work via callbacks (Step 3). (3) `cached_user`/`cached_organization` are denormalized snapshots feeding search + feeds. (4) The `published` scope, not just a boolean, defines visibility everywhere.

---

# Step 3 — How an article + attached data are handled on change

**Trigger entry points:** `Articles::Creator` (`app/services/articles/creator.rb:26`), `Articles::Updater` (`updater.rb:15`), `Articles::Unpublish` → Updater (`unpublish.rb:14`), `Articles::Destroyer` (`destroyer.rb:5`). Controllers call these (`articles_controller.rb`, API controllers).

**Inline (synchronous) on save — callbacks in `article.rb`:**
- `before_save`: `set_cached_entities` (`:305`), `set_all_dates` (`:306`), `calculate_base_scores` (`:308`), `set_caches` (path/cached_user) (`:310`), `detect_language` (`:311`); `Taggable#sync_tags_array` (`taggable.rb:57`).
- `before_destroy :before_destroy_actions, prepend` (`:313` → `:1408`): inline `bust_cache(destroying:true)` then enqueues `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker` for sibling articles. `WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains` also prepended (`webpage_trackable.rb:8`).
- `after_save`: `bust_cache` (`:316` → `:1663`: `purge` Fastly inline + `async_bust` + touch actor), `collection_cleanup` (`:317`), `generate_social_image` (`:318`), `generate_context_notes` (`:319`).
- `after_update_commit`: `update_notifications` (`:321` → `Notification.update_notifications`), `update_notification_subscriptions` (`:324`), `update_dependent_embeds_if_key_info_changed` (`:341`), `regenerate_summary_if_content_changed` (`:343`).
- `Updater` inline post-save: `Mentions::CreateAll.call` (`updater.rb:79`), `remove_all_notifications` + `EdgeCache::BustUser` on unpublish (`:83,:100`), `refresh_auto_audience_segments` (`:54`).
- `Destroyer` inline post-destroy: `Notification.remove_all` for the article and its (now-nullified) comments (`destroyer.rb:15-19`), `EdgeCache::BustUser` (`:21`).

**Asynchronous (Sidekiq) — fanned out from `after_commit`:**
- `async_score_calc` → `Articles::ScoreCalcWorker` (`:338` → `:1122`)
- `touch_collection`, `enrich_image_attributes` → `Articles::EnrichImageAttributesWorker` (`:1700`), `detect_code_block_languages` → `Articles::DetectCodeBlockLanguagesWorker` (`:1706`)
- `enqueue_generate_embedding` → `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker` (`:334`/`:1808`)
- `recompile_organization_pages` → `Organizations::RecompilePagesWorker` (`:345`/`:1729`)
- `bust_cache` → `async_bust` → `Articles::BustCacheWorker` (`:1692` → `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call`, `bust_cache_worker.rb:8`)
- `create_conditional_autovomits` → `Articles::HandleSpamWorker` (`:1682`)
- `generate_social_image` → `Images::SocialImageWorker` (`:1675`)
- `WebpageTrackable`: `SyncWebpageReferencesWorker` on update, `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker` on destroy (`webpage_trackable.rb:15,24`)
- `LiquidEmbeddable`: `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:28`)
- `AlgoliaSearchable`: `AlgoliaSearch::SearchIndexWorker` via `trigger_sidekiq_worker` (`searchable_article.rb:37`)

**Effect layers:** caches/Fastly (`EdgeCache::BustArticle` `services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb:9` — purges article, user, org, home/tag/profile pages); feeds (score recompute, org page recompile); notifications (`update_notifications`, `Mentions::CreateAll`); counters (`counter_culture :user/:organization`); search (Algolia + pg tsvector trigger + semantic embedding).

---

# Step 4 — Every dependent of the Article contract (grouped, individual file:line)

### Request handling (controllers)
- `articles_controller.rb:6` — CRUD via Creator/Updater/Destroyer; `set_article`
- `api/v0/articles_controller.rb` & `api/v1/articles_controller.rb` / `concerns/api/articles_controller.rb` — REST article CRUD
- `stories_controller.rb:48` — `Article.includes(:user).find_by(slug:…)` show page
- `stories/feeds_controller.rb:21` — feed JSON + pinned article
- `stories/tagged_articles_controller.rb:25` — tag feed
- `stories/pinned_articles_controller.rb:12` — `PinnedArticle.get`
- `stories/articles_search_controller.rb:9` — article search page
- `comments_controller.rb:36` — `@commentable.is_a?(Article)`, discussion lock
- `reactions_controller.rb:12` — react to article by `article_id`
- `api/v1/reactions_controller.rb` — API reactions on articles
- `moderations_controller.rb:22` — `Moderations::ArticleFetcherService`
- `article_approvals_controller.rb:3` — `Article.find` approve
- `tag_adjustments_controller.rb:17` — adjust article tags via service
- `video_states_controller.rb:17` — `Article.find_by(video_code:)`
- `page_views_controller.rb:39` — `Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker`
- `dashboards_controller.rb:37` — `target.articles`
- `sitemaps_controller.rb:26` — `Article.published.from_subforem`
- `profile_pins_controller.rb`, `discussion_locks_controller.rb`, `video_states_controller.rb`, `videos_controller.rb`, `trends_controller.rb`, `search_controller.rb`, `sidebars_controller.rb`, `pages_controller.rb`, `community_controller.rb`, `events_controller.rb`, `image_uploads_controller.rb`, `billboards_controller.rb`, `bottom_items_controller.rb`, `feeds/sources_controller.rb`, `users_controller.rb`, `users/settings_controller.rb`, `admin/articles_controller.rb`, `admin/reactions_controller.rb`, `admin/tools_controller.rb`, `admin/users_controller.rb`, `admin/welcome_controller.rb`, `admin/feedback_messages_controller.rb`, `ahoy/email_clicks_controller.rb`, and `concerns/api/{analytics,comments,organizations,readinglist,trends,users,videos}_controller.rb` — each reads/derives articles for its area.

### Background work (workers)
- `articles/bust_cache_worker.rb:8` — `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call`
- `articles/bust_multiple_caches_worker.rb:10` — bust sibling article paths
- `articles/score_calc_worker.rb:11` — `article.update_score`
- `articles/handle_spam_worker.rb:13` — spam recheck + `update_score`
- `articles/enrich_image_attributes_worker.rb:14` — image attrs + re-bust
- `articles/detect_code_block_languages_worker.rb:16` — code-lang detect + re-bust
- `articles/generate_summary_worker.rb:13` — AI summary (gates on score/comment_score)
- `articles/generate_context_note_worker.rb:8` & `generate_freeform_context_note_worker.rb:10` — context notes
- `articles/update_dependent_embeds_worker.rb:7` — re-render LiquidEmbedReferences
- `articles/publish_worker.rb:9` — scheduled publish
- `articles/quality_reaction_worker.rb:20` — quality reaction `max_score`
- `articles/label_cleanup_worker.rb:20` — automod label cleanup
- `articles/update_article_activity_worker.rb:35` — `article_activity` upkeep
- `articles/update_page_views_worker.rb:37` — page view counts (guards `published?`)

# Step 5 — How article identity & visibility are guarded

When background/cross-service code operates on an article, three layers of guarding appear:

**1. Existence (record still present).** Async workers fetch by id and bail if gone — the standard pattern after a destroy:
- `articles/bust_cache_worker.rb:5-6` (`find_by(id:)` + `return unless article`), and identically `score_calc_worker.rb:9`, `handle_spam_worker.rb:9`, `enrich_image_attributes_worker.rb:9`, `detect_code_block_languages_worker.rb:11`, `generate_summary_worker.rb:14`, `generate_context_note_worker.rb:9`, `generate_freeform_context_note_worker.rb:10`, `rating_votes/assign_rating_worker.rb:9`, `follows/update_points_worker.rb:9`, `notifications/milestone_worker.rb:9`, `notifications/subforem_change_notification_worker.rb:9`, `update_user_interest_embedding_worker.rb:8`.
- `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call` guards `return unless article` (`bust_article.rb:10`).
- `Images::SocialImageWorker:8` uses `find` (raises if gone) — note: less defensive than the `find_by` pattern.

**2. Current + published (visibility).** The `published` scope is the canonical visibility gate: `published: true AND published_at <= Time.current` (`article.rb:411`). Built on by every feed/query: `feeds/basic.rb:13`, `feeds/latest.rb:11`, `feeds/tag.rb:13`, `homepage/articles_query.rb:43`, `active_threads_query.rb:28`, `email_digest_article_collector.rb:63`, `sitemaps_controller.rb:26`, `metrics/record_daily_usage_worker.rb:8`. Explicit `published?` re-checks before acting:
- `generate_article_embedding_worker.rb:7` (`article.published?`), `articles/update_page_views_worker.rb:38` (`article&.published?`), `concepts/classify_record_worker.rb:14` (`!record.published?` → skip), `slack/messengers/article_published.rb:10` (`published && published_at > 10.minutes.ago`), `edge_cache/bust_article.rb:50` (`return unless article.published` before tag-page bust).

**3. Search/index eligibility.** `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle#indexable` (`searchable_article.rb:42`): `published && score.positive? && published_at < Time.current` — and `indexable_changed?` (`:46`) gates re-indexing. Semantic embedding has its own gate: `eligible_for_semantic_embedding?` (`article.rb:1026`) + the `after_commit` `published? && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present?` (`:336`).

**4. Authorization (request path).** `ArticlePolicy` — `manage?` requires `record.published? && !record.scheduled?` (`article_policy.rb:73`); `revoke_publication?` requires `@record.published?` (`:92`); `feed?`/`create?`/`update?` (`:40,:53,:66`).

**Identity nuances:** `scheduled?` (`article.rb:663`: `published_at && published_at > now`) distinguishes future-dated; `cached_user`/`cached_organization` (`set_cached_entities` `:1615`) snapshot the actor's identity into the article so feeds/search don't re-query. Destroyer relies on comment ids being captured *before* destroy because `comments` are `:nullify`'d (`destroyer.rb:11`).

# Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

The ticket changes "how it stores and tears down attached data" — i.e. the association set and their `dependent:` strategies. Ranked by risk:

**🔴 Highest — silent data loss or orphans on teardown:**
1. **`comments` `dependent: :nullify`** (`article.rb:171`). Destroyer *depends* on this: it caches `article.comments.ids` (`destroyer.rb:11`) before destroy specifically because comments survive, then removes their notifications (`:17-19`). Any change to `:destroy`/`:delete_all` silently breaks `Destroyer`, `lib/data_update_scripts/20200825095512_nullify_orphaned_comments_by_article.rb`, and `Notification.remove_all` for comments. Re-verify `spec/services/articles/destroyer_spec.rb`.
2. **`tag_adjustments` with no `dependent:`** (`article.rb:188`). Already orphans on destroy; if the rework adds cascade, must confirm `TagAdjustment` has no counter_culture/callbacks that need it, and check `tag_adjustment_creation_service.rb`.
3. **`reactions` `dependent: :destroy`** (`reactable.rb:5`) + `counter_culture`. Destroying reactions triggers per-reaction callbacks and counter sync; `reaction_counter_sync_worker.rb:80`, `reactions/update_relevant_scores_worker.rb`, `Reactable#sync_reactions_count` all assume the current relationship.
4. **`rating_votes`/`polls` `dependent: :destroy`** (`article.rb:183,:187`) — comments in code explicitly note they're `:destroy` *because* of counter_culture/cascade; switching strategy loses tallies.
5. **`before_destroy_actions` + `BustMultipleCachesWorker`** (`article.rb:1408`) — runs `prepend: true` and reads `user.article_ids`/`organization.article_ids`; reordering associations or destroy hooks can change what's busted.

**🟠 High — async fan-out keyed on the article after commit:**
6. `EdgeCache::BustArticle` (`bust_article.rb:9`) — purges article/user/org/home/tag/profile; depends on `tag_list`, `published`, `user`, `organization` still being readable.
7. `WebpageTrackable` destroy hooks (`webpage_trackable.rb:8,9`) — `capture_webpage_domains` (prepend) + `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker`; if `webpage_references` strategy changes, domain scores drift.
8. `LiquidEmbeddable` (`liquid_embeddable.rb:5,28`) + `update_dependent_embeds_worker.rb` — embeds referencing the article.
9. `notifications`/`notification_subscriptions`/`context_notifications` `:delete_all` (`article.rb:177,178,172`) — `Updater#remove_all_notifications` (`updater.rb:83`) and `Destroyer` (`destroyer.rb:15`) both also remove notifications manually; double-handling means a strategy change can leave dangling notification rows.
10. Search: `AlgoliaSearchable` (`searchable_article.rb`) + pg `reading_list_document` trigger (`article.rb:359`) + semantic embedding (`:329,:334`) — index entries must be removed/updated on teardown.

**🟡 Medium — read/derive paths that assume associations load:**
11. Feeds/ranking (`feeds/*`, `homepage/articles_query.rb`, `active_threads_query.rb`) — rely on `published`, `featured`, counters, `cached_tag_list`.
12. AI assessors (`services/ai/*`), badges (`services/badges/*`), digest (`email_digest_article_collector.rb`), analytics (`analytics_service.rb`, `admin/*`).
13. `concept_memberships`/`trend_memberships` `:destroy` (`article.rb:192,195`) + concept workers.

**Must re-verify after the change (regardless of area):** `Articles::Destroyer`, `Articles::Updater` (unpublish path), `EdgeCache::BustArticle`, all `dependent:`-affected child models' counters, the orphan-cleanup data scripts, Algolia + pg-trigger + embedding indexing, and `before_destroy_actions` ordering (`prepend: true` interacts with `WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains`).
